ip_outcome_note: Card name 'Croagunk', Darkness type, Basic subtype, flavor text matches. National Pokedex no. 453, Sinnoh Toxic Mouth Pokemon. Confirmed.

trivia_bullets.text: "Once diluted, its poison becomes medicinal. This Pokémon came into popularity after a pharmaceutical company chose it as a mascot." One of the few species with an explicitly stated corporate-commercial relationship built into its lore. Consistent across multiple Pokedex entries.

Cross-print distinction: BUS-54 (Burning Shadows, wave 14 trivia complete) is the Brock's-Croagunk anime-association print. This Fusion Strike print (no. 165/264, Nagomi Nijo art) stages Croagunk in dense tropical foliage — distinct framing from BUS-54. A third inventory print also exists: Sword & Shield base set no. 123/202.

Illustrated by Nagomi Nijo, a TCG illustrator and semifinalist in the Pokemon Card Game Illustration Grand Prix. Fusion Strike was one of Nijo's more prolific sets: Pansage, Croagunk, and Goodra all appear as Nijo illustrations in swsh8.
sources: Bleeding Cool: Nagomi Nijo artist spotlight

trivia_bullets.text: Croagunk (no. 453) is a dual-type Poison/Fighting Pokemon introduced in Generation IV (Diamond/Pearl, 2006, Sinnoh). Evolves into Toxicroak (no. 454). TCG types as Darkness in most Generation IV-era prints.

related_cards.name: Croagunk
related_cards.note: no. 54/147, wave 14 trivia complete, Brock's-Croagunk anime association
